rationale: The citing paper uses Bechwati and Siegal (2005) as background evidence for general claims that return-policy conditions and postpurchase disconfirming information can affect perceived risk and return behavior. These mentions appear in the literature framing section, but the cited work is not carried into the paper's core model specification, estimation approach, field-experiment design, or result interpretation. The substantive development of the paper relies mainly on CLV and resource-allocation frameworks rather than directly building on the cited paper's prechoice-process mechanism.
